explanation: The statement "We'd want to try to get some understanding..." implies a request for information. This LTL property ensures that whenever an information request (infoRequest) begins (i.e., transitions from false to true), the robot will provide explanations for both its visited locations (explainVisited) and measurement origins (explainMeasurements) in the next time step. Additionally, it ensures that if an information request is currently active, these explanations are provided immediately.

The statement implies that when information is requested (infoRequest), the robot should explain both the locations it has visited (explainVisited) and the origins of its measurements (explainMeasurements). This translates into a global property where infoRequest implies the conjunction of the two desired explanations.

The statement expresses a desire to eventually gain understanding about the robot's past locations and measurement origins. This is captured by the "eventually" operator (F) applied to the conjunction of the two desired explanations.

The statement implies a request for information. This LTL property ensures that whenever an information request (infoRequest) begins (i.e., transitions from false to true), the robot will provide its visited locations (returnVisited) and measurement origins (returnMeasurementOrigins) in the next time step. Additionally, it ensures that if an information request is currently active, this information is provided immediately.

The statement implies that when information is requested (infoRequest), the robot should return both the locations it has visited (returnVisited) and the origins of its measurements (returnMeasurementOrigins). This translates into a global property where infoRequest implies the conjunction of the two desired data returns.

The statement expresses a desire to eventually obtain information about the robot's past locations and measurement origins. This is captured by the "eventually" operator (F) applied to the conjunction of the two desired data returns.
This captures the requirement that globally, if an information request (infoRequest) is made, then in the very next time step, the robot should explain its visited locations and measurement origins.

This robustly captures the requirement that whenever an information request (infoRequest) begins, the explanations for visited locations and measurement origins are provided in the next two steps (for the first part) or in the very next step if the request is already active.
This captures the requirement that globally, if an information request (infoRequest) is made, then in the very next time step, the robot should return its visited locations and measurement origins.

This robustly captures the requirement that whenever an information request (infoRequest) begins, the data returns for visited locations and measurement origins are provided in the next two steps (for the first part) or in the very next step if the request is already active.

Additionally, if an information request is currently active, this explanation is provided immediately. This ignores the measurement part.

Additionally, if an information request is currently active, this explanation is provided immediately. This ignores the visited locations part.
